meta.title: Truvis — Smarter used-car inspections
meta.description: Truvis is a used-car inspection app that helps you know exactly what you are buying. Join the waitlist for early access.

hero.eyebrow: The pre-purchase inspection in your pocket
hero.headline: A buyer paid €7,200 for a car with a €900 problem he couldn't see.
hero.subheadline: Truvis is a pocket inspector that walks you through a used car, flags what matters, and tells you what to negotiate — before you pay.
hero.phoneAlt: Truvis app showing a used-car inspection in progress with a severity summary for the buyer.

problem.headline: Buyers lose an average of €640 to hidden issues they couldn't see on a test drive.

body.lede: Used cars hide problems that a casual test drive won't surface — worn clutches, misaligned frames, electronic gremlins, history odometer rolls. The ones that do get caught are usually caught at the mechanic, after the money has already changed hands.
body.stat: Roughly 1 in 3 private-party used cars has at least one undisclosed issue costing more than €500 to fix. The average buyer walks away from a handshake deal €640 poorer than they expected.
body.stakes: On a €12,000 car, that is a 5 % hit before you've even driven it home. It is also entirely preventable — if you know what to look for.

problem.sourceNote: Source: placeholder data — replaced with sourced statistics in Epic 5.

inspectionStory.headline: Six moments, one inspection.
inspectionStory.subheadline: A walk through the six things Truvis looks for on every used car. The exact moments that separate a good deal from a disaster.

scene1.narrative: A 2017 Ford Focus with the 1.0 EcoBoost has a documented coolant-line failure pattern after 60,000 miles and a known timing-chain tensioner wear signature. Truvis already knows those exact faults before you open the bonnet, so you walk in with the same shortlist a seasoned mechanic carries in their head.
scene1.featureBenefit: Know the faults for this exact engine before you open the bonnet.

scene2.narrative: Not every finding is a dealbreaker. A worn wiper is a €12 Sunday fix. A seeping head gasket is a €2,200 emergency. Truvis grades every observation green, amber, or red against real repair costs, so the weight of each finding matches its real-world impact on your wallet.
scene2.featureBenefit: Every finding weighed in euros, not vague warnings.

scene3.narrative: A young family and a weekend hobbyist tolerate risk differently. Truvis asks three short questions about your budget, your daily-driver needs, and how much of a fix-up project you actually want, and then recalibrates every severity threshold to match. The same car can be a pass for one buyer and a walk-away for another.
scene3.featureBenefit: Thresholds adjusted to your budget and your appetite for repairs.

scene4.narrative: The seller sees a neutral progress screen. No severity colours, no running tally, no red flags flashing over your shoulder. Truvis keeps the full inspection private on your device until you choose what to share, so your negotiation leverage stays yours until the moment you decide to use it.
scene4.featureBenefit: The seller sees an in-progress screen. You keep the leverage.

scene5.narrative: Some findings are not negotiable. A compromised airbag, a cut-and-shut chassis weld, a VIN that does not match the logbook. When Truvis hits one, the app raises a Hard Stop and tells you to walk away on the spot. The feature exists so that a good deal never quietly becomes a dangerous one.
scene5.featureBenefit: Safety-critical findings trigger an immediate walk-away signal.

scene6.narrative: At the end of the inspection Truvis hands you a short, itemised negotiation brief. Every amber finding gets a line, a real repair cost estimate, and a suggested price delta. You leave the driveway with a clear counter-offer instead of a gut feeling, and the seller sees numbers instead of haggling.
scene6.featureBenefit: An itemised counter-offer built from real repair costs, ready to send.

socialProof.headline: A used-car market that punishes buyers who don't look.

quote.text: The used-car market is fundamentally asymmetric. Sellers always know more than buyers, and that gap is where most of the money goes.
quote.context: V1 placeholder — replaced with a real testimonial or industry-publication quote in Epic 5.

faq.headline: Questions worth asking before you buy.

scope.question: What exactly does Truvis do?
scope.answer: Truvis walks you through a used-car inspection on your phone, flags the parts most likely to fail on your specific model and year, and gives you a severity-rated summary and a list of issues worth negotiating on before you hand over the money. It is designed to sit in your pocket during the test drive and the walk-around.

vs-mechanic.question: Does Truvis replace a professional pre-purchase inspection?
vs-mechanic.answer: No, and it is not designed to. A qualified mechanic with a lift and diagnostic scan tools will always see more than a phone in a car park. Truvis is the step before that: it tells you whether a car is worth booking a mechanic for at all, and which specific things to ask the mechanic to check.

privacy.question: What does Truvis do with my data?
privacy.answer: Truvis stores your inspection history on your device by default. If you opt into cloud sync, inspection data is stored in an EU region only. We never sell your data, we never share it with dealerships, and you can delete your history any time from the app settings.

cost.question: How much does it cost?
cost.answer: Pricing is being finalised before launch. Sign up to the waitlist and you will be among the first to know. Early signups will receive preferential introductory pricing.

platforms.question: Which phones does Truvis run on?
platforms.answer: Truvis will launch on iOS and Android. The waitlist covers both, and we will notify you as soon as your platform is ready to download.

accuracy.question: How accurate are Truvis's severity ratings?
accuracy.answer: Severity is calibrated against the documented fault histories for each specific model and model year, combined with the visible and audible signals Truvis asks you to check. It is a decision-support tool, not a diagnostic certification, so always combine it with a test drive and, for expensive cars, a professional inspection.

data-retention.question: How long does Truvis keep my inspection data?
data-retention.answer: Inspection data stays on your device indefinitely unless you delete it. If you enable cloud sync, you can delete every record from the app settings. Deletion is immediate, verifiable, and permanent. See our privacy policy for the full data lifecycle.
